description_long: This object is a detailed model of a spiny lobster, featuring a segmented, armored exoskeleton with a rough, textured surface. The body is elongated, with a prominent cephalothorax and a tapering abdomen ending in a broad, fan-shaped tail. It has long, slender walking legs and two very long, curved antennae extending from the head. The coloration is a mix of beige, tan, and light brown, with subtle mottling and speckling across the shell. The eyes are small and set on short stalks at the front of the head, and there are no large claws present. The lobster is displayed on a flat, dark rectangular base, emphasizing its naturalistic posture and anatomical details.
description: A spiny lobster with a segmented, armored shell, long legs, and extended antennae, displayed on a dark base. Its surface is rough and mottled in beige and brown tones.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the lobster's body, segmented tail, and the curvature of its long antennae.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the lobster's head, forward-projecting antennae, and symmetrical arrangement of legs.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the segmentation of the abdomen and the arch of the antennae.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the broad, fan-shaped tail and the alignment of the legs and antennae extending backward.
